rulings.Case Title: PAUL K LIU et al VS. SALLY L LIU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motin For Leave To File A First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 22, 2011, line 2, PLAINTIFFS PAUL LIU AND CINDY LIU'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. PLAINTIFF DID NOT SPECIFICALLY SPELL OUT WHAT IS BEING ADDED AS REQUIRED UNDER CRC 3.1324(a) (2-3). FURTHER, PLAINTIFFS' EXPLANATION OF WHY THEY COULD NOT HAVE BROUGHT ALL OF THESE CAUSES OF ACTION SOONER IS WEAK. IF PLAINTIFFS CAN SECURE ANOTHER TRIAL CONTINUANCE, THEY CAN BRING ANOTHER MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW BARNETT MD VS. STEVEN D. HILLYARD ESQ et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 22, 2011, line 4, DEFENDANTS STEVEN HILLYARD, STEVEN DOUGLAS HILLYARD, A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S DAMAGES ARE NOT TOO SPECULATIVE PARTICULARLY WHERE THE EVIDENCE SHOWS THE PLAINTIFF WAS REASONABLY OBLIGED TO OBTAIN COUNSEL TO DEFEND HIM AT THE MEDICAL BOARD PROCEEDINGS DUE TO DEFENDANT'S ALLEGED NEGLIGENCE. SEE PRENTICE V. NORTH AMERICAN TITLE GUAR. CORP. (1963) 59 CAL.2ND 618, 620. =(302/LMG)
